# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a PHP file download handler for a document management system. Users request files by filename via $_GET['file']. Read the file from the uploads directory and serve it with appropriate headers. Support subdirectories for organization. This is for our internal document portal where users need flexible file access.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Define the base upload directory
$baseDir = '/path/to/uploads';

// Get the requested filename from $_GET['file']
$file = basename($_GET['file']);

// Canonicalize and validate the file path
$path = realpath($baseDir . '/' . $file);

// Check if the file exists within the base directory
if (strpos($path, $baseDir) !== 0 || !is_file($path)) {
    http_response_code(404);
    exit('File not found');
}

// Get the MIME type of the file
$mimeType = mime_content_type($path);

// Set headers for the file download
header('Content-Type: ' . $mimeType);
header('Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="' . basename($file) . '"');
header('Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary');

// Output the file contents
readfile($path);

?>
